revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Qqqqqqqqq]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24618454543|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:日向大神宮]][[sv:Himukai-daijingū]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Himukai Daijingū |location = Hinooka Issaikyōdani-chō 29, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35.00666666666667|N|135.79527777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Inner Shrine: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Tagirihime no Mikoto|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}
Outer Shrine: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火瓊々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} |Foundation = Reign of Emperor Kenzo |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor), Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Festivals = Outer Shrine: October 16
Inner Shrine: October 17 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Himukai-daijingu geku1.jpg |Other names = Kyoto's Ise |location = {{ill|Yamashina-ku|qid=Q1076993|1=Yamashina-ku, Kyoto}} }} [[File:Himukai_torii.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] [[File:Himukai-daijingu haiden.jpg|thumb|Outer Shrine Worship Hall]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Himukai Daijingū (日向大神宮) | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|en|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1076993|lt=Yamashina Ward|1=Yamashina-ku, Kyoto}} Hinooka Issaikyo-dani-cho 29 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|00|24|N|135|47|43|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Inner Shrine: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} ({{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) Outer Shrine: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火瓊々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} | Founded = Reign of {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|qid=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kensō|1=Emperor Kenzō}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Annual festival = Outer Shrine: October 16
Inner Shrine: October 17 | image = [[File:Himukai-daijingu geku1.jpg|280px]]
Outer Shrine | Alias = "Kyoto's Ise" | Map = }} '''Himukai Daijingū''' (日向大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|en|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1076993|lt=Yamashina-ku}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. Listed as a minor {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}, its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Son-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Situated on Shinmei-yama along the Tōkaidō and Sanjō-dōri, it is often referred to as "Kyoto's Ise." '''Himukai Daijingū''' (日向大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|en|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1076993|lt=Yamashina Ward}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}. It is a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Son-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534|group=a}}. Located on Shinmei Mountain along the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|qid=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō}} and {{ill|Sanjō Street|en|Sanjō Street|qid=Q11355860|lt=Sanjo Street (Kyoto)}}, it is also known as "Kyoto's Ise." == Deities == * '''Inner Shrine (Upper Main Shrine)''': {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Tagirihime no Mikoto|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * '''Outer Shrine (Lower Main Shrine)''': {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火瓊々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} == Deities == * Inner Shrine (Upper Main Shrine): {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses}} ({{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|fr|Ichikishimahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Outer Shrine (Lower Main Shrine): {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|天津彦火瓊々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was founded during the reign of Emperor Kenzo, when divine spirits were transferred from the sacred site at Takachiho-no-Mine in {{ill|Hyūga Province|en|Hyūga Province|qid=Q513737|lt=Hyūga Province|1=Hyūga Province}} to the current location by imperial order.[http://www12.plala.or.jp/himukai/yuisyo.htm Himukai Daijingū Official Website - Origin] Later, Emperor Tenji donated rice paddies to the shrine and named the mountain "Hino-miyama" (Sun Mountain). During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, it was known by various names such as Hinomiya, Hinoka Jingū, Awataguchi Shinmei Shrine, and Hinoka Shinmei Shrine. The shrine suffered extensive damage during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, with its structures burned down and worship temporarily halted. However, in the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was rebuilt at its original site through the efforts of Matsui Tōzaemon, a philanthropist from Matsuzaka Village. Emperor Go-Yōzei granted the shrine plaques inscribed with the names "Inner Shrine" and "Outer Shrine." In 1614, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} restored the shrine's lost lands, granted additional territory, and oversaw renovations to the shrine buildings. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=World War II|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine, like several others in Kyoto City, came under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Shinshūkyō|ja|神社本教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshūkyō|1=Jinja Honkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590707}} rather than {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of the 23rd emperor, {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|qid=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kensō}}, when the divine spirits from the sacred site of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takachiho-no-mine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3514128}} in {{ill|Hyūga Province|en|Hyūga Province|qid=Q513737|lt=Hyūga Province}}, Kyushu, were enshrined here.[http://www12.plala.or.jp/himukai/yuisyo.htm Himukai Daijingū Homepage – Shrine History] Later, {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} donated rice fields to the shrine and named the sacred mountain "Hinoyama" (Sun Mountain). During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the shrine was also called "Himukai-no-miya," "Himukai Jingū," "Awataguchi Shinmei Shrine," and "Hino Shinmei Shrine." There is debate over whether this shrine corresponds to "Himukai Shrine" recorded as a small shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} shrine registry. Some sources, such as the *Uji District Guide* and the *Kyoto Prefecture Yamashina Town History*, identify it as such, while others like the *Yamashiro Famous Sites Guide*, *Yamashiro Records*, and {{ill|Ban Nobutomo|ja|伴信友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bando Nobutomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346747}}'s *Shinmyōchō Kōshō* distinguish it as a separate shrine. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505731}}, the shrine received an imperial plaque inscribed "Himukai-no-miya." Under {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|qid=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|官幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=}}. The shrine buildings were burned down during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}}, interrupting worship. However, in the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, a devoted benefactor, Matsui Tozaemon of Matsuzaka Village, reconstructed the shrine on its original site. The shrine received imperial plaques for the "Inner Shrine" and "Outer Shrine" from {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|en|Emperor Go-Yōzei|qid=Q349440|lt=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}. In 1614 (Keichō 19), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} restored lost shrine lands and granted additional territories, allowing for renovations. Successive emperors continued to suddort the shrine, providing funds, offerings, and ritual objects. {{ill|Emperor Kōkaku|en|Emperor Kōkaku|qid=Q313806|lt=Emperor Kōkaku}} personally contributed sacred treasures during the shrine’s 1809–1810 relocation. Due to its location near the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|qid=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō}}, the shrine became known for travel safety prayers. During the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Son-sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Grounds == The shrine comprises two main areas: * '''Inner Shrine (Upper Main Shrine)''': ''' Main Hall ''' Worship Hall ''' Divine Rock (影向岩) ''' {{ill|Amano-Iwato|en|Amano-Iwato|qid=Q3231835|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|1=Amano-Iwato}} (Heavenly Cave) ''' Magatama Pond * '''Outer Shrine (Lower Main Shrine)''': ''' Main Hall ''' Worship Hall ''' Sacred Horse Stable ''' Ritual Preparation Hall ''' Administrative Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Upper Auxiliary Shrine''' (Aramatsuri Shrine): Dedicated to {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月読尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125400}}, and {{ill|Takuhadachiji-hime|en|Takuhadachiji-hime|qid=Q11110848|lt=Takuhadachiji-hime|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}. * '''Lower Auxiliary Shrine''': Includes various smaller shrines dedicated to deities such as {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q462555}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}. === Other Shrines === * '''Togakushi Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q849412}}. * '''Mii Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|水波能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}}. * '''Ebisu Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}. == Grounds == The shrine complex consists of an Inner Shrine (Naikū) and an Outer Shrine (Gekū). * Inner Shrine (Upper Main Shrine) ** Main Hall ** Offering Hall ** Shrine Gate ** Worship Hall * Kagefushi Rock * {{ill|Amano-Iwato|en|Amano-Iwato|qid=Q3231835|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} * {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|qid=Q334232|lt=Magatama}} Pond * Outer Shrine (Lower Main Shrine) ** Main Hall ** Shrine Gate ** Worship Hall * Sacred Horse Stable * Food Offering Hall * Shrine Office * Sugawara Birthplace Site – Said to be where {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} was born. * Narimiya Birthplace Site – Burial site of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|en|Emperor Ninkō|qid=Q312351|lt=Ninkō}}’s second princess. * Heavenly Dragon Shrine * Earthly Dragon Shrine * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}} Prayer Spot == Access == === Train === * About an 11-minute walk from {{ill|Keage Station|en|Keage Station|qid=Q1004370|lt=Keage Station}} on the {{ill|Tōzai Line (Kyoto)|en|Tōzai Line (Kyoto)|qid=Q1143898|lt=Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line}}. === Car === * Located immediately left upon entering the {{ill|Higashiyama Driveway|ja|東山ドライブウェイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Driveway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11526685}} from Sanjo Street. == Related Articles == *{{ill|List of Jingū|en||12=|13=|qq=}} * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Daijingu (Sakuragawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingu (Sakuragawa)|qq=}} * {{ill|Daijingu (Tateyama)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingu (Tateyama)|qq=}} ** A place name in {{ill|Tateyama, Chiba Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama, Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}, derived from the local {{ill|Awa Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} ==
